entry.title: U形管
entry.content:
Me: I had a new question about diffusion phenomenon.

A U-shaped pipe containing a piece of semipermeable membrane in the middle part of it is now filled up with two types of solutions. The left side is filled up with glucose solution, while the right side is filled up with sucrose solution. We define that they are of the same volume and mass fraction(not molarity, instead, the same mass).

Now explain what will happen under the following two circumstances:

  • The semipermeable membrane allows water and glucose to go across itself.
  • The semipermeable membrane allows water to go across itself.

Just state it simply with the facts of diffusion phenomenon.

GPT-4o:

Let's break down what would happen in each case based on the principles of diffusion and osmosis.

  1. When the membrane allows water and glucose to pass through:

    • Water and glucose molecules can both move across the membrane, so they will tend to diffuse towards the side with lower concentrations of water or glucose until equilibrium is reached.
    • Since both sides start with equal volumes and mass fractions of glucose and sucrose, water movement will likely be minimal.
    • Glucose molecules will diffuse from the side with a higher concentration of glucose to the side with lower glucose concentration until they are evenly distributed on both sides.
    • Sucrose cannot pass, so it remains in place, meaning that glucose alone equalizes across both sides, while sucrose concentration remains different.

  2. When the membrane allows only water to pass:

    • Water molecules will diffuse across the membrane to balance the osmotic pressure difference caused by the presence of sucrose and glucose.
    • Since sucrose cannot pass, it creates a higher osmotic pressure on the sucrose side, drawing water towards it.
    • This will lead to water moving from the glucose side to the sucrose side, increasing the volume of the sucrose solution side and decreasing the volume on the glucose side.
    • Glucose and sucrose concentrations will change due to this water movement, but they won’t diffuse across the membrane since glucose cannot move to the sucrose side in this setup.

Me:

Okay, but I guess I am confused.

Q1: How do the diffusion works? Does it depend on molarity or mass fraction? Or I am going to ask, "If a piece of membrane has solutions of the same mass fraction or of the same molarity(but of different solutes) on its two sides. Only water moves. What is the result respectively?"

Q2: What if membrane allows solutes to pass? Am I going to say sucrose solution is also solvent as it allows glucose to dissolve?

GPT-4o:

Great questions! Diffusion and osmosis can be tricky, especially when considering concentration measures like mass fraction and molarity. Let’s go through each of these concepts to clarify.

Q1: How does diffusion work? Does it depend on molarity or mass fraction?

Diffusion depends primarily on molarity rather than mass fraction. Here’s why:

  1. Diffusion and Molarity: Diffusion is driven by concentration gradients in terms of particles (molecules or ions). Molarity measures concentration based on the number of moles per unit volume of solution, which reflects how many particles are present. Particles diffuse from areas of high molarity (high particle concentration) to areas of low molarity until equilibrium is reached.

  2. Mass Fraction: Mass fraction (or mass percentage) measures the amount of solute relative to the total mass of the solution. However, different solutes have different molar masses, so equal mass fractions do not correspond to equal numbers of particles.

For example, a glucose solution and a sucrose solution of the same mass fraction will not have the same molarity because sucrose molecules are heavier than glucose molecules. This means they don’t have the same number of particles per liter of solution, so diffusion would occur based on the molarity difference.

Scenario Analysis with Water Movement Only

If the semipermeable membrane only allows water to pass and you have solutions with:

  • Equal Mass Fractions: Water will move from the side with the lower molarity to the side with the higher molarity (i.e., towards the side with fewer molecules per unit volume).

  • Equal Molarities: If both sides have the same molarity (the same particle concentration) but different solutes, no net water movement will occur. This is because the osmotic pressures are balanced as each side has an equal number of particles.

Q2: What if the membrane allows solutes to pass?

If the membrane allows both solutes to pass (in this case, glucose and sucrose), diffusion of the solutes will occur across the membrane:

  1. Direction of Movement: Glucose and sucrose will each diffuse according to their concentration gradients until equilibrium is reached on both sides. Both solutes will spread across the membrane until their individual molarities equalize on both sides.

  2. Solvent-Solute Relationship: In solutions, water is typically considered the primary solvent. However, if glucose and sucrose are able to pass through the membrane, we could think of them as dissolving in each other’s solutions. In this sense, the term “solvent” refers more broadly to the ability of one substance (water) to dissolve both glucose and sucrose, facilitating the movement of particles across the membrane. However, sucrose doesn't become a solvent in a strict chemical sense; it simply diffuses into the space, mixing with water and glucose to equalize concentrations.

In summary:

  • Diffusion relies on molarity (number of particles), not mass fraction.
  • Water movement depends on osmotic pressure differences, which relate to molarity.
  • When solutes can pass, they diffuse individually to balance concentration on both sides, acting as if they “dissolve” in each other’s presence if allowed by the membrane.

Me: Thanks a lot. So in my original questions, which side of the U-shaped pipe will finally has a higher liquid level (for both two circumstances)?

(P.S. I'm going to say, "the same mass fractions" is emphasized.)

GPT-4o:

Let’s go through each case in your original question with the emphasis on "the same mass fractions."

Recap of Key Points:

Since the solutions have the same mass fraction of glucose and sucrose, the molarity (particle concentration) will differ. This is because sucrose molecules are larger and heavier than glucose molecules. Therefore:

  • The sucrose side will have fewer particles (lower molarity) than the glucose side, even though their mass fractions are the same.
  • This difference in particle concentration affects osmotic pressure, which influences the direction of water movement.

Analysis of Liquid Level in Each Case

Case 1: Membrane allows water and glucose to pass

  1. Water Movement: Because both sides have equal mass fractions but different molarities, water will initially tend to move from the lower molarity side (sucrose) to the higher molarity side (glucose) to balance osmotic pressure.

  2. Glucose Movement: Glucose can also diffuse across the membrane. It will tend to move from the glucose-rich side to the sucrose-rich side until it reaches equilibrium.

  3. Final Liquid Levels:

    • Water initially moves towards the glucose side, raising its liquid level.
    • As glucose equilibrates across both sides, the molarity difference reduces, which reduces further water movement.
    • The final liquid levels will likely be similar on both sides due to the balancing effect of both water and glucose movement.

Case 2: Membrane allows only water to pass

  1. Water Movement: Since only water can move and not glucose or sucrose, water will flow from the glucose side (higher molarity) to the sucrose side (lower molarity) due to osmotic pressure differences.

  2. Final Liquid Levels:

    • This water movement will increase the liquid level on the sucrose side and decrease it on the glucose side.
    • Ultimately, the sucrose side will have a higher liquid level as water moves to balance the concentration of particles across the membrane.

Summary:

  • In Case 1 (water and glucose can pass): Both sides will likely end up with similar liquid levels.
  • In Case 2 (only water can pass): The sucrose side will have a higher liquid level due to osmotic movement of water towards the lower molarity side.

Me: Hey, I guess you are wrong! Water goes to higher molarity side!

GPT-4o:

You’re absolutely right, and I apologize for the oversight! Water indeed moves toward the side with the higher molarity (higher concentration of solute particles), not the lower. Let’s correct the explanation based on this:

Corrected Analysis of Liquid Levels

Since the glucose and sucrose solutions have equal mass fractions but different molarities (glucose side having a higher molarity due to smaller molecule size), the osmotic pressure will be higher on the glucose side. Thus, water will flow toward the glucose side in both cases.

Revised Results for Each Case

Case 1: Membrane allows both water and glucose to pass

  1. Water Movement: Water moves towards the glucose side because it has a higher osmotic pressure due to the higher particle concentration.

  2. Glucose Movement: Glucose will also diffuse from the glucose side to the sucrose side until equilibrium is reached.

  3. Final Liquid Levels:

    • Initially, water will raise the liquid level on the glucose side.
    • As glucose diffuses across to equalize its concentration, the osmotic pressure difference diminishes, stabilizing the water movement.
    • Ultimately, both sides may end up with similar liquid levels due to the combined effect of water and glucose diffusion balancing out.

Case 2: Membrane allows only water to pass

  1. Water Movement: Water moves from the sucrose side (lower molarity) toward the glucose side (higher molarity).

  2. Final Liquid Levels:

    • Since glucose cannot move, the osmotic pressure difference remains, so water will continue moving to the glucose side.
    • The glucose side will end up with a higher liquid level.

Corrected Summary:

  • In Case 1 (water and glucose can pass): The liquid levels will likely stabilize and be similar on both sides.
  • In Case 2 (only water can pass): The glucose side will have a higher liquid level as water continues moving toward it due to its higher molarity.

Thanks for catching that error!

Me: ...Hey. I'm going to say I've just found that you also made a mistake when answering the question in the first circumstance. You see, as for glucose, its molarity on the sucrose side is lower(just for glucose itself). So it travels to the other side. And now the water didn't actually flow.

But after the glucose transports, we can see for all of the solvents. The molarity is higher on the right side.

We can see that if we assume that there should be 2 molecules of glucose and 1 molecules of sucrose(as we all know the mass ratio). After passing through the membrane, there is only one molecule of glucose on the left side, and there is one molecule of glucose and one molecule of sucrose. Thus, the sucrose side has a higher molarity since it contains the same volume of water but more molecules compared with the glucose side.

Therefore, the water travels, to the sucrose side. And it turns out that the sucrose side has a higher water level.

Isn't this theory convincing?

GPT-4o:

Your analysis is absolutely correct! Let’s go through your reasoning step by step, as it’s a great way to understand the diffusion process here.

Updated Analysis for Case 1 (Water and Glucose Can Pass)

  1. Initial Glucose Diffusion:

    • Since glucose can diffuse across the membrane, it will initially move from the glucose side (left), where its concentration is higher, to the sucrose side (right), where its glucose concentration is lower.
    • After some glucose has moved to the sucrose side, the glucose concentration difference will start to decrease.

  2. Osmotic Pressure Considerations After Glucose Diffusion:

    • Now, the sucrose side has both sucrose and some added glucose, while the glucose side has less glucose than before.
    • Because both solutes contribute to the total molarity, the sucrose side ends up with a higher total particle concentration (higher molarity) after glucose diffuses.
    • This difference in total molarity results in a higher osmotic pressure on the sucrose side.

  3. Water Movement Due to Osmotic Pressure:

    • Since water moves towards the side with a higher osmotic pressure, water will flow from the glucose side to the sucrose side.
    • This movement of water raises the liquid level on the sucrose side.

Final Outcome

  • In Case 1 (membrane allows both water and glucose to pass): The sucrose side ends up with a higher liquid level. This is because, after glucose diffuses, the sucrose side has a higher total molarity, causing water to flow in that direction.

  • In Case 2 (membrane allows only water to pass): As previously discussed, the glucose side initially has a higher osmotic pressure, so water flows towards it, causing the glucose side to have a higher liquid level.

Summary of the Updated Findings

  • Case 1: Sucrose side ends up with a higher water level.
  • Case 2: Glucose side ends up with a higher water level.

Your reasoning is indeed convincing and provides a clearer, more accurate understanding of what’s happening in each case. Thank you for catching that detail!

Me: Yes! Thank you very much.

GPT-4o: You're very welcome! I'm glad we could work through the details together, and it's impressive how you analyzed it so thoroughly. Questions like these show a great grasp of diffusion and osmotic processes, so keep up the excellent work! If you have more questions or another scenario to analyze, I’m here to help. 😊

entry.title: 不等式全家桶(抽象记忆版)
entry.content:
君の高考は。\Huge \color{lightblue}君の高考は。\\ 君の高考は。

记忆口诀要求: 简单, 全面, 抽象.

基本不等式

普通形式:

a+b2≥ab\Huge \frac{a+b}{2}\ge\sqrt{ab} 2a+b​≥ab​

其中 a,b∈R+a,b\in \R_+a,b∈R+​. 当且仅当 a=ba=ba=b 时, 等号成立.

平方形式:

a2+b2≥2ab\Huge a^2+b^2\ge2ab a2+b2≥2ab

其中 a,b∈Ra,b\in \Ra,b∈R. 当且仅当 a=ba=ba=b 时, 等号成立.

均值不等式

二元形式:

11a+1b≤ab≤a+b2≤a2+b22\large\frac{1}{\displaystyle\frac{1}{a}+\frac{1}{b}}\le\sqrt{ab}\le\frac{a+b}{2}\le\sqrt{\displaystyle\frac{a^2+b^2}{2}} a1​+b1​1​≤ab​≤2a+b​≤2a2+b2​​

其中 a,b∈R+a,b\in \R_+a,b∈R+​. 当且仅当 a=ba=ba=b 时, 所有等号成立.

调和 ≤\le≤ 几何 ≤\le≤ 算数 ≤\le≤ 平方 小小调酒三瓶🍷🍸🍹

多元形式:

1∑i=1n1xi≤∏i=1nxin≤∑i=1nxin≤∑i=1nxi2n\Large\frac{1}{\displaystyle\sum_{i=1}^{n}\frac{1}{x_i}} \le \sqrt[n]{\prod_{i=1}^{n}x_i}\le \frac{\displaystyle\sum_{i=1}^{n}x_i}{n}\le\sqrt{\frac{\displaystyle\sum_{i=1}^{n}x_i^2}{n}} i=1∑n​xi​1​1​≤ni=1∏n​xi​​≤ni=1∑n​xi​​≤ni=1∑n​xi2​​​

其中 x1⋯xn∈R+x_1\cdots x_n\in \R_+x1​⋯xn​∈R+​. 当且仅当 x1=⋯=xnx_1=\cdots=x_nx1​=⋯=xn​ 时, 所有等号成立.

柯西不等式

四元形式:

(x2+y2)(a2+b2)≥(xa+by)2\large (x^2+y^2)(a^2+b^2)\ge(xa+by)^2 (x2+y2)(a2+b2)≥(xa+by)2

其中 a,b,x,y∈Ra,b,x,y\in \Ra,b,x,y∈R. 当且仅当 ax=by\displaystyle\frac{a}{x}=\frac{b}{y}xa​=yb​ 时, 等号成立. (注意!可以取全体实数!)

方和积 ≥\ge≥ 积和方 方盒鸡 大雨 鸡何方? 📦🐔→☔→🐔❓

多元形式:

(∑i=1nxi2)(∑i=1nyi2)≥(∑i=1nxiyi)2\Large\left(\sum_{i=1}^{n}x^2_i\right)\left(\sum_{i=1}^{n}y^2_i\right)\ge\left(\sum^{n}_{i=1}x_iy_i\right)^2 ⎝⎜⎛​i=1∑n​xi2​⎠⎟⎞​⎝⎜⎛​i=1∑n​yi2​⎠⎟⎞​≥⎝⎜⎛​i=1∑n​xi​yi​⎠⎟⎞​2

其中 x1⋯xn,y1⋯yn∈Rx_1\cdots x_n, y_1\cdots y_n\in\Rx1​⋯xn​,y1​⋯yn​∈R. 当且仅当 x1y1=⋯=xnyn\displaystyle\frac{x_1}{y_1}=\cdots=\frac{x_n}{y_n}y1​x1​​=⋯=yn​xn​​ 时, 等号成立.

权方和不等式

四元形式:

a2x+b2y≥(a+b)2x+y\large \frac{a^2}{x}+\frac{b^2}{y}\ge\frac{(a+b)^2}{x+y} xa2​+yb2​≥x+y(a+b)2​

其中 a,b,x,y∈R+a,b,x,y\in\R_+a,b,x,y∈R+​. 当且仅当 ax=by\displaystyle\frac{a}{x}=\frac{b}{y}xa​=yb​ 时, 等号成立.

分和 ≥\ge≥ 总和, 上面分别平方. 粪大肿, 尚方宝剑! 💩🙀, 🗡❗❗

扩展形式(注意次数):

amxm−1+bmym−1≥(a+b)m(x+y)m−1\large \frac{a^m}{x^{m-1}}+\frac{b^m}{y^{m-1}}\ge\frac{(a+b)^m}{(x+y)^{m-1}} xm−1am​+ym−1bm​≥(x+y)m−1(a+b)m​

其中 a,b,x,y∈R+a,b,x,y\in\R_+a,b,x,y∈R+​. 当且仅当 ax=by\displaystyle\frac{a}{x}=\frac{b}{y}xa​=yb​ 时, 等号成立.

分子次数正好比分母多 111.

多元形式:

∑i=1nxi2yi≥(∑i=1nxi)2∑i=1nyi\Large \sum^{n}_{i=1}\frac{x_i^2}{y_i}\ge\frac{\left(\displaystyle\sum_{i=1}^{n}x_i\right)^2}{\displaystyle\sum_{i=1}^ny_i} i=1∑n​yi​xi2​​≥i=1∑n​yi​⎝⎜⎛​i=1∑n​xi​⎠⎟⎞​2​

其中 x1⋯xn,y1⋯yn∈R+x_1\cdots x_n, y_1\cdots y_n\in\R_+x1​⋯xn​,y1​⋯yn​∈R+​. 当且仅当 x1y1=⋯=xnyn\displaystyle\frac{x_1}{y_1}=\cdots=\frac{x_n}{y_n}y1​x1​​=⋯=yn​xn​​ 时, 等号成立.

多元扩展形式:

∑i=1nximyim−1≥(∑i=1nxi)m∑i=1nyim−1\Large \sum^{n}_{i=1}\frac{x_i^m}{y_i^{m-1}}\ge\frac{\left(\displaystyle\sum_{i=1}^{n}x_i\right)^m}{\displaystyle\sum_{i=1}^ny_i^{m-1}} i=1∑n​yim−1​xim​​≥i=1∑n​yim−1​⎝⎜⎛​i=1∑n​xi​⎠⎟⎞​m​

其中 x1⋯xn,y1⋯yn∈R+x_1\cdots x_n, y_1\cdots y_n\in\R_+x1​⋯xn​,y1​⋯yn​∈R+​. 当且仅当 x1y1=⋯=xnyn\displaystyle\frac{x_1}{y_1}=\cdots=\frac{x_n}{y_n}y1​x1​​=⋯=yn​xn​​ 时, 等号成立.

函数的凹凸性

直观地来看, 一个函数的图像凸起来或者凹下去就是函数的凹凸性.

如图, f(x)=exf(x)=e^xf(x)=ex 是下凸函数, 而 f(x)=ln⁡xf(x)=\ln xf(x)=lnx 是上凸函数.

形式化地, 对一个函数定义其凹凸性:

如果在区间 [l,r][l,r][l,r] 上 f(x)f(x)f(x) 是连续的, 且在 (l,r)(l,r)(l,r) 内具有二阶导数, 那么:

  • 当 f′′(x)>0f''(x)>0f′′(x)>0 时, 函数在区间 [l,r][l,r][l,r] 上是下凸的.
  • 当 f′′(x)<0f''(x)<0f′′(x)<0 时, 函数在区间 [l,r][l,r][l,r] 上是上凸的.

直观地说, 就是斜率的单增和单减.

琴生不等式

二元形式:

f(x1+x22)≤f(x1)+f(x2)2\Large f\left(\frac{x_1+x_2}{2}\right)\le \frac{f(x_1)+f(x_2)}{2} f(2x1​+x2​​)≤2f(x1​)+f(x2​)​

其中 f(x)f(x)f(x) 是下凸的, 即 f′′(x)>0f''(x)>0f′′(x)>0. x1,x2∈Rx_1,x_2\in\Rx1​,x2​∈R, 当且仅当 x1=x2x_1=x_2x1​=x2​ 时取等.

如果函数是上凸的, 不等号反向.

这个不等式成立也是很直观的:

如图, 这是一个下凸的函数.

因为它是向下"凹"的, 所以连接 A,BA,BA,B 的线段一定处于 A,BA,BA,B 之间的函数图像上方.

多元形式:

f(∑i=1nxin)≤∑i=1nf(xi)n\Large f\left({\sum_{i=1}^{n}x_i}\over{n}\right)\le \frac{\sum_{i=1}^{n}f(x_i)}{n} f⎝⎛​n∑i=1n​xi​​⎠⎞​≤n∑i=1n​f(xi​)​

其中 f(x)f(x)f(x) 是下凸的, 即 f′′(x)>0f''(x)>0f′′(x)>0. x1⋯xn∈Rx_1\cdots x_n\in\Rx1​⋯xn​∈R, 当且仅当 x1=⋯=xnx_1=\cdots=x_nx1​=⋯=xn​ 时取等.

如果函数是上凸的, 不等号反向.

entry.title: 别腐数学-重要的数学式子
entry.content:
Möbius反演典中典式子

反演结论:

∑d∣nμ(d)=ε(n)=[n=1]\Large\sum_{d|n}\mu(d)=\varepsilon(n)=[n=1] d∣n∑​μ(d)=ε(n)=[n=1]

∑i=1n∑j=1m[gcd⁡(i,j)=k]=∑i=1⌊nk⌋∑j=1⌊mk⌋[gcd⁡(i,j)=1]=∑i=1⌊nk⌋∑j=1⌊mk⌋∑d∣gcd⁡(i,j)μ(gcd⁡(i,j))=∑d=1min⁡(⌊nk⌋,⌊mk⌋)μ(d)∑i=1⌊nk⌋[d∣i]∑j=1⌊mk⌋[d∣j]=∑d=1min⁡(⌊nk⌋,⌊mk⌋)μ(d)⌊ndk⌋⌊mdk⌋\large{ \sum_{i=1}^n\sum_{j=1}^m\left[\gcd\left (i,j\right)=k\right] }\\ =\large{ \sum_{i=1}^{\left\lfloor\frac{n}{k}\right\rfloor}\sum_{j=1}^{\left\lfloor\frac{m}{k}\right\rfloor}\left[\gcd\left (i,j\right)=1\right] }\\ =\large{ \sum_{i=1}^{\left\lfloor\frac{n}{k}\right\rfloor}\sum_{j=1}^{\left\lfloor\frac{m}{k}\right\rfloor}\sum_{d|\gcd(i,j)}\mu(\gcd(i,j)) }\\ =\large{ \sum_{d=1}^{\min(\left\lfloor\frac{n}{k}\right\rfloor,\left\lfloor\frac{m}{k}\right\rfloor)}\mu(d)\sum_{i=1}^{\left\lfloor\frac{n}{k}\right\rfloor}[d|i]\sum_{j=1}^{\left\lfloor\frac{m}{k}\right\rfloor}[d|j] }\\ =\large{ \sum_{d=1}^{\min(\left\lfloor\frac{n}{k}\right\rfloor,\left\lfloor\frac{m}{k}\right\rfloor)}\mu(d)\left\lfloor\frac{n}{dk}\right\rfloor\left\lfloor\frac{m}{dk}\right\rfloor } i=1∑n​j=1∑m​[gcd(i,j)=k]=i=1∑⌊kn​⌋​j=1∑⌊km​⌋​[gcd(i,j)=1]=i=1∑⌊kn​⌋​j=1∑⌊km​⌋​d∣gcd(i,j)∑​μ(gcd(i,j))=d=1∑min(⌊kn​⌋,⌊km​⌋)​μ(d)i=1∑⌊kn​⌋​[d∣i]j=1∑⌊km​⌋​[d∣j]=d=1∑min(⌊kn​⌋,⌊km​⌋)​μ(d)⌊dkn​⌋⌊dkm​⌋

矩阵的行列式

det⁡(A)=∑σ∈Snsgn⁡(σ)∏inai,σ(i)\Large\det(A)=\sum_{\sigma\in S_n}\operatorname{sgn}(\sigma)\prod_{i}^{n}a_{i,\sigma(i)} det(A)=σ∈Sn​∑​sgn(σ)i∏n​ai,σ(i)​

其中, σ\sigmaσ 是 [1,n][1,n][1,n] 的一个全排列, SnS_nSn​ 是所有长度为 nnn 排列的集合.

积式的系数 sgn⁡(σ)\operatorname{sgn}(\sigma)sgn(σ) 被定义为 σ\sigmaσ 中逆序对的个数作指数的 −1-1−1 的幂, 形式化地, 记 ccc 为排列中的逆序对个数, 那么

sgn⁡(σ)=(−1)c\large \operatorname{sgn}(\sigma)=(-1)^c sgn(σ)=(−1)c

所以 sgn⁡(σ)\operatorname{sgn}(\sigma)sgn(σ) 与逆序对个数的奇偶性有关.

等比数列求和

P=∑i=0npip⋅P=∑i=1n+1pi(p−1)P=pn+1−p0P=pn+1−1p−1\Large{ P=\sum_{i=0}^{n}p^i\\ p\cdot P=\sum_{i=1}^{n+1}p^i\\ (p-1)P=p^{n+1}-p^{0}\\ P=\frac{p^{n+1}-1}{p-1} } P=i=0∑n​pip⋅P=i=1∑n+1​pi(p−1)P=pn+1−p0P=p−1pn+1−1​

主定理

对递推关系为

T(n)=aT(nb)+f(n)T(1)=1\Large T(n)=aT\left(\frac{n}{b}\right)+f(n)\\ \Large T(1)=1 T(n)=aT(bn​)+f(n)T(1)=1

的分治算法求解复杂度

前半部分 aT(nb)\Large aT\left(\frac{n}{b}\right)aT(bn​) 是分解子问题的复杂度计算次数, f(n)\Large f(n)f(n) 是合并子问题的计算次数.

aT(nb)=Θ(nlog⁡ba)f(n)=Θ[f(n)]\Large aT\left(\frac{n}{b}\right)=\Theta\left(n^{\log_ba}\right)\\ \Large f(n)=\Theta\left[f(n)\right] aT(bn​)=Θ(nlogb​a)f(n)=Θ[f(n)]

谁更大就取谁, 相等的时候 T(n)=Θ[f(n)log⁡n]\Large T(n)=\Theta\left[f(n)\log n\right]T(n)=Θ[f(n)logn].

如何比较谁更大? 将 f(n)\Large f(n)f(n) 化为幂形式 f(n)=nc\Large f(n)=n^cf(n)=nc, 那么 c=log⁡nf(n)\Large c=\log_nf(n)c=logn​f(n).

当 c>log⁡ba\Large c>\log_bac>logb​a 时, f(n)\Large f(n)f(n) 更大.

当 c=log⁡ba\Large c=\log_bac=logb​a 时, 二者相等.

当 c<log⁡ba\Large c<\log_bac<logb​a 时, aT(nb)\Large aT\left(\frac{n}{b}\right)aT(bn​) 更大.

导

(u±v)′=u′±v′(uv)′=u′v+uv′(uv)′=u′v−uv′v2\Large(u\pm v)'=u'\pm v'\\ \Large(uv)'=u'v+uv'\\ \Large\left(\frac{u}{v}\right)'=\frac{u'v-uv'}{v^2} (u±v)′=u′±v′(uv)′=u′v+uv′(vu​)′=v2u′v−uv′​

欧拉函数

φ(n)=n∏pi−1pi\Large \varphi(n)=n\prod\frac{p_i-1}{p_i} φ(n)=n∏pi​pi​−1​

其中 n=∏piki,pi∈primen=\prod p_i^{k_i}, p_i\in \mathtt{prime}n=∏piki​​,pi​∈prime.

φ(n)\varphi(n)φ(n) 定义为小于 nnn 且与 nnn 互质的数的个数.

entry.title: 极昼-2024军训游记
entry.content:
推歌环节

个人博客就是好, 可以直接嵌入Iframe.

两首音乐都来自音乐人 Anthemics.

Day 0

早上报到也没啥活动, 认识了和蔼可亲的老班李晨老师. @CyanWind 要我上去和他撕名牌( @Tzy090420 说我脸倒过来看像邓超). 熟悉了下班里的好朋友们.

确实好多熟人诶, ZBR好友开大会.

大家都做了自我介绍, 很欢乐!

晚, 讲座: 高中各科学习方法.

有点恼火, 听了半天最后最喜欢科目的化学生物老师都没上台, 不过还是有学到有用的东西.

Day 1

早: 动员大会. 体育馆地板上哗哗铺开的都是迷彩绿, 武警教官个个人高马大的煞是骇人.

见到了小蔡教官, CCQ! 乍一看身高不高, 却是很干练很结实的样子. 迷彩服在肋骨下扎上宽皮带束着, 全身都漾出精神的气息. 生着一副棱角刚硬的五角脸, 被太阳晒得黧黑, 鼻翼下面还有一道浅浅的伤痕. 绛紫色的嘴唇看上去就言语寡少. 后来我发现, 等他走到太阳底下, 脸庞就会泛出淡淡的潮红, 面色看上去极红润, 就没有那样的黧黑色了.

出门时被堵在体育馆门口了, 看到小蔡教官在摆弄对讲机, 可以触屏很高级的样子.

中午也有难忘的事, 陪明德老同学CYC去买帽子, 遇到一个女生没带现金, 借了她二十.

她笑着追了我好远问我是几班的.

我跑出去10几米远, 给她追回来了.

不知为何, 我离开时秋风洒下了更多的黄叶.

下午站军姿. 大概提了下要求就散了. 中考久坐真的害人! 第一天站的时候腰是很酸的.

果然CCQ就是不善言谈的那类人啊, 开口组织语言都花了好久, 硬汉都是这样的吗?

晚: 校规校纪. 特别关注了校规校纪小本子上的奇怪内容. 每个表象之后都藏着一段故事, 奇葩的规矩也不例外.

Day 2

因为怕低血糖, 提前一天找 @Tzy090420 拿了一块小饼干.

早: 吃了小饼干, 发现是胡椒味的辣嗓子.

跑到训练场站队. 第一次见到早晨6点多的JZ, 雾霭里混着矢车菊蓝色, 有那样美得窒息的感觉.

CCQ: (对班主任晨哥说) 有没有选去国旗班的同学?

晨哥: 没.

CCQ: (对大家说) 有没有想去的?

沉默以后CCQ就开始巡视队列挑人. 由于饼干的后劲很大我极想咳嗽, 刚好又碰到小蔡踱步到我背后.

一阵寒意从背后漫散开来.

我: (挺了一下腰杆)

CCQ: 这个.

我: (腰软下来) 我?

CCQ: 嗯.

所以就出列了.

去国旗班站了下军姿例行, 见到了可爱的ZQH教官! 是山东人, 性格很俏皮, 稚气未泯, 说话时也很爱打趣抖机灵.

后来了解到ZQH去比武大赛曾经因为差0.2分而错失国赛名额, 好可惜...但是他一副无所谓的样子, 说什么人有得必有失, 看淡就好. 后来晚上睡觉, 我也想过这个问题, 不过这就是哲学讨论了, 还是不赘述.

上午国旗班训练气氛也很轻松, 又见到了来自梅州的小张教官ZYM.

ZYM: 大家休息一下, 我给大家表演个喝水.

我们:(鼓掌) 好!好!

ZYM:(拧开瓶盖喝了一小口水)

ZYM: 表演完了.

哈哈哈.

后来军训组织学拉歌, 山东汉子ZQH哑着嗓子表演了《团结》.

学长学姐来选了下人, 给我选进去了. 后来经过讨论, 我为了OI还是退出了.

嗯, 有得必有失.

晚上光顾着想早上的事了, 讲了些啥?

Day 3

户外拉练! 其实就是出去犁头尖山逛了一圈.

知道了小蔡是潮州人, 确实很典型的干练的潮汕风格.

炸裂消息: CCQ担任狙击手, 帅炸了!

路上为了给小蔡台阶下, 吼得很大声.

小蔡: (回头看到我吼得很卖力, 竖了两个大拇指) 大家都跟着他喊!

一路上气氛都像艳阳一样高涨! 教官们都在很卖力地组织拉歌(虽然CCQ没有一句着调).

山顶的景色也很清新, 金黄金黄的叶片抖落下的残影都溅在我们身上. 教官黧黑的面容也好像与阳光相似相溶, 甚是和谐.

可惜国旗班队列不讲拉歌号子, 一路上我都在乱喊.

所以回来以后嗓子哑了.

下午学长分享, 又全是文科, 你们能不能喜欢一下数理化?

学长学姐都很会分配时间, 又能参与社团又能考好大学, 嗯?

晚上听了校长的校史校情, 感觉JZ这片厚土真的无比沉重温润! 笑点: 主任对校庆90周年纪念水的无比执着. 后面学唱了歌, 很开心, 但是嗓子真不行.

Day 4

记忆里抹不掉的烙印.

今天开始练队列行进. CCQ要我出来喊号子.

忽的就有一种及其沉重的担子砸在我肩上. 所以我每次喊的时候都是憋住隔膜, 努力顶出来所有的气, 吼得满场回响.

哑了, 哑了, 倏地就有一种畅快淋漓的感觉.

休息时. 大家都在打趣说我的声音像擎天柱.

CCQ哑着嗓子问: 谁的嗓子哑了?

我很不好意思地应了一声.

我第一次看到他绽出来一个富有少年气的笑容, 眼角挤出的皱纹浮游在被晒得发红的皮肤上.

他伸出右手, 要我和他握手.

宽大的手掌很粗糙, 握着有种浅浅的厚实感, 他于是有力地上下摆动了一下手臂, 接着就从裤袋里摸出一盒润喉糖, 倒了两颗在我伸出的两只手心里.

他回头看看, 问还有谁嗓子哑了.

我只听到太阳刮过树叶溅出的风声.

王老吉润喉糖, CCQ给的

练齐了! 大家回头一看

"哇啊!!--"

CCQ: (笑着鼓掌)

喔对了, CCQ笑的时候 , 是把嘴唇侧边水平地拉开, 上下方向并没有什么弧度, 形成一个近似矩形的框框,里面洁白的牙齿露出来, 极像青春期男生不好意思咧开嘴笑的样子, 看上去有点傻傻的.

下午去了校史馆和杨殷故居, 认真学了好多历史知识! 早期革命者真是不容易啊.

进去之前就有了CCQ数学名场面: 我的数学是体育老师教的.

一班的好多同学都去参加了校史历史宣讲, 他们也挺厉害的, 讲的时候很有条理.

晚上也在发呆. 随便记了些讲座的要点. 讲座之前ZQH在二楼组织拉歌, 吼得也很使劲, 嗓子也是哑着的. 我看他笑着拿下话筒, 捂着嘴咳嗽一下, 迷彩帽子都在振动. 其实也不免有点心疼, 果然军人服从命令和执行任务时, 从不会有什么遗留, 总是酣畅而洋洋洒洒.

所以讲座讲了啥来着?

Day 5

被CCQ提前拉出来单独练了, 目的是带大家一起做好.

CCQ看到我头上趴了一只马蜂, 把帽子摘下来使劲儿一拍: "我X!没打死!"

哈哈哈!

组织大家练了齐步跑! 刚开始立定口号老喊错, 真是愧疚! 小蔡特意要我给他下口令来纠正, 看到他标准的动作还真是有被吓到.

CCQ看我嗓子哑了还给我扔水.

JZ军训时分发给老师和教官饮用的阿尔卑斯矿泉水, CCQ分了我一瓶, 天气太热已经喝完

(不是音游人吗这个空拍子找不到?)

大家练了好几趟终于跑齐了.

大家回头一看: (笑着鼓掌)

CCQ(站在我背后): "哇啊!!---"

过了一会.

CCQ: "欸?怎么不袜了?"

哈哈哈!

CCQ还表演了单手握拳俯卧撑! 真是帅爆了.

中午起床后就有 @123czh 和小蔡极限拉扯一幕.

哈哈哈.

晚上在大家的帮忙下弄好了班级后面滂臭的垃圾! 谢谢CZY, ZZX和WZJ!

后面好像是急救讲座, 帮忙收好了器材.

Day 6

早: 会操表演彩排, 帮大家拿水杯跑得满头大汗.

晚: 校规考试, 因为有特地研究男女同学相处的规定并意识到了这是考试重点所以没挂.

Day 7

好不舍得啊!

跑完了会操表演, 班长LJK把写好的感谢信递给小蔡. 小蔡利索地敬了个标准的军礼.

那盒润喉糖, 他从裤袋里翻出来发给大家, 那样朴素的笑容传染了所有人.

才一个星期, 怎么有种很深的触动呢?

牢骚: 为什么不让我上台叠被子!

下午: 家长会, 很不好意思地赶出了一封信给老妈. 窝在香山书房和 @NOT_Anymore 一起读了一下午《地理中国》.

回家的路上, 背后的云彩又结成了浪潮的形状.

后日谈

Day1借钱的女孩特地找我还了钱.

妈妈很担心我的嗓子, 特意买了胖大海给我泡着喝, 过了三天便不哑了.

尝到了好学姐 @cookiesL 的手信, 谢谢菲姐姐!

特地记了小蔡领子上章的图案, 算了算他大概服役了八年.

和 @123czh 聊了才知道他是突击小队队长, 徒手擒过敌, 应该也立了不少功吧. 说实话很崇拜.

果然, 非凡夫俗子也.

夜里躺在床上, 还会想起ZQH的那句话.

教室后面的垃圾也清得干干净净了! 真好.

这些飘渺的碎片的记忆在意识里随波逐流, 我怕它们走丢了.

就让你们再拼起来, 重塑一个极昼似的暖房吧.

entry.title: For Oh For Stories-404小故事
entry.content:
CZL来纪中的第14个晚上, 大家在聊天.

我:(编故事)有一种生物住在公路旁边, 头上长着标志.

我:如果有危险它就会嚎叫, 提醒司机注意避让.

TZY:快说谢谢你标志狼.

我:谢谢你标志狼.

CZL:破大防

过了1919810天

我:哈哈菜籽油.

CZL:爆炸

ZBR来纪中的第1145141919810天.

我:躺在床上腐

CZL:坐在床边的阶梯上腐

CZL:你的脸倒过来看好像邓超.

我:???

TZY:把WQP拉过来

WQP:哈哈邓超.

我:破防

晚上.

CZL:讲个笑话我笑了算你们赢.

WQP:(笑话)

CZL:不笑.

TZY:(笑话)

CZL:不笑.

我:(已经笑晕)

TZY:ZBR讲一个.

我:我不会讲.

过了一会.

我:笑话系统.

CZL:噗哈哈哈哈哈哈哈笑话系统哈哈哈哈哈.

怀念ZTY特辑

WQP:在体育场南门.

TZY:哈哈哈哈.

我:哈哈哈哈.

CZL:哈哈哈哈.(笑点解析:汉语拼音首字母)

我:无穷朴素对数表.

TZY:哈哈哈哈.

WQP:破防

CZL:哈哈哈哈哈...蛤?

CZL:你们那种笑话不好笑, 我要听ZBR上次那种零帧起手的笑话(指笑话系统).

我:菜籽油.

CZL:破防

CZL:Hush.

CZL:曼波~

我:唱的什么.

CZL:Duang~

我:唱的什么.

CZL:欧爷.

(ZBR回家看到了孤高之人诗歌剧版本)

(回到JZ)

CZL:Hush.

我:曼波~

CZL:Don't speak.

我:欧爷!

(夏令营结束后114514天)

我:When you spit your venom keep it shut I hate it!

我:Duang!

(笑话夜晚过去后)

TZY:知道为什么CZL害怕法国人吗?

我:为什么.

CZL:为什么.

WQP:为什么.

TZY:因为法克油.

我:噗哈哈哈哈哈哈哈哈哈.

WQP:噗哈哈哈哈哈哈哈哈哈哈哈哈哈.

CZL:噗哈哈哈哈哈哈哈...蛤?

entry.title: 数论:Miller-Rabin素性检验/Pollard-Rho算法分解质因数
entry.content:
I.Miller-Rabin素性检验

二次探测定理:

对于奇素数 ppp, 当整数 aaa 与 ppp 互质时, 有费马小定理:

ap−1≡1(modp)a^{p-1}\equiv 1\pmod p ap−1≡1(modp)

p−1p-1p−1 是偶数, 所以:

(ap−12)2≡1(modp)⇒(ap−12)2−1≡0(modp)⇒(ap−12+1)(ap−12−1)≡0(modp)⇒ap−12≡±1(modp)\begin{matrix} &&(a^{\frac{p-1}{2}})^2\equiv1 \pmod p\\ &\Rarr&(a^{\frac{p-1}{2}})^2-1\equiv0 \pmod p\\ &\Rarr&(a^{\frac{p-1}{2}}+1)(a^{\frac{p-1}{2}}-1)\equiv0 \pmod p\\ &\Rarr&a^{\frac{p-1}{2}}\equiv\pm1 \pmod p \end{matrix} ​⇒⇒⇒​(a2p−1​)2≡1(modp)(a2p−1​)2−1≡0(modp)(a2p−1​+1)(a2p−1​−1)≡0(modp)a2p−1​≡±1(modp)​

则当 ppp 是奇素数时, 同余方程 x2≡1(modp)x^2\equiv1\pmod px2≡1(modp) 的解只有 ±1\pm1±1 (称为平凡因子).

当 p=2p=2p=2 时, 可以得出方程 x2≡1(modp)x^2\equiv1 \pmod px2≡1(modp) 的解只有 111.

假设需要检验的数为 xxx.

当 xxx 为偶素数时, 仅有一种情况 x=2x=2x=2, 特判即可.

当 xxx 为奇素数时, 对于 a∈[1,x)∩Z+a\in[1,x)\cap\Z_+a∈[1,x)∩Z+​, 一定有费马小定理 ax−1≡1(modx)a^{x-1}\equiv1\pmod xax−1≡1(modx);

所以当 ∃a0∈[1,x)∩Z+,a0x−1≡1(modx)\exist a_0\in [1,x)\cap\Z_+, a_0^{x-1}\not\equiv1\pmod x∃a0​∈[1,x)∩Z+​,a0x−1​​≡1(modx), 显然 xxx 不为素数.

反之, 如果 ∀a∈[1,x)∩Z+,ax−1≡1(modx)\forall a\in[1,x)\cap\Z_+,a^{x-1}\equiv1\pmod x∀a∈[1,x)∩Z+​,ax−1≡1(modx), 那么 xxx 为素数.

但全部判别一次实现上是不允许的, 因此需要概率上优化.

因为 xxx 是一个奇数(除 222 外的偶数皆为合数, 可以直接判断), 那么 x−1x-1x−1 是偶数, 所以 x−1x-1x−1 可以被表示为 2rd2^rd2rd.

我们假定 xxx 是素数, 有费马小定理 ax−1≡1(modx)a^{x-1}\equiv 1\pmod xax−1≡1(modx).

于是有(这里的 aaa 满足 a∈[1,x)∩Z+,a∤xa\in[1,x)\cap\Z_+,a\nmid xa∈[1,x)∩Z+​,a∤x):

ax−1≡1(modx)⇒a2rd≡1(modx)a^{x-1}\equiv1\pmod x\Rarr a^{2^rd}\equiv1\pmod xax−1≡1(modx)⇒a2rd≡1(modx).

根据二次探测定理有 a2r−1d≡±1(modx)a^{2^{r-1}d}\equiv\pm1\pmod xa2r−1d≡±1(modx).

如果 a2r−1d≡1(modx)a^{2^{r-1}d}\equiv1\pmod xa2r−1d≡1(modx), 那么又会有 a2r−2d≡±1(mod⁡x)a^{2^{r-2}d}\equiv\pm1(\operatorname{mod}x)a2r−2d≡±1(modx).

以此类推, 所以数列 R={a20d,a21d,⋯ ,a2r−1d,a2rd}R=\{a^{2^0d},a^{2^1d},\cdots,a^{2^{r-1}d},a^{2^rd}\}R={a20d,a21d,⋯,a2r−1d,a2rd} (每一项都是前一项的平方) 在模 xxx 运算以后得到的结果 SSS 形如以下四类:

(1): S={1,⋯ ,1}S=\{1,\cdots,1\}S={1,⋯,1}.(序列都是 111)

(2): S={⋯ ,−1,1,⋯ 1}S=\{\cdots,-1,1,\cdots\,1\}S={⋯,−1,1,⋯1}.(中途某一项是 −1-1−1, 而后都是 111)

(3): S={⋯ ,d,1,⋯ ,1}S=\{\cdots,d,1,\cdots,1\}S={⋯,d,1,⋯,1}.(中途有一项是 111, 但它的前一项是 d≠±1d\neq\pm1d​=±1)

(4): S={⋯ ,f}S=\{\cdots,f\}S={⋯,f}.(结尾 f≠1f\neq 1f​=1)

对于情况(1)和(2), aaa 都不能证明 xxx 是合数.

对于情况(3), 可以发现 d2≡1(modx)d^2\equiv1\pmod xd2≡1(modx) 且 d≠±1d\neq\pm 1d​=±1, 那么就说明 ddd 是 111 的非平凡因子, 由二次探测定理可证明 xxx 是合数.

对于情况(4), 显然 ax−1≡1(modp)a^{x-1}\not\equiv 1\pmod pax−1​≡1(modp), 不满足费马小定理, xxx 是合数.

若选择 kkk 个底数 aaa, Miller-Rabin 的错误概率为 4−k4^{-k}4−k 是可以接受的, 平均时间复杂度为 O(klog3n)O(k log^3 n)O(klog3n).

Jim Sinclair发现的数列 A={2,325,9375,28178,450775,9780504,1795265022}A=\{2, 325, 9375, 28178, 450775, 9780504, 1795265022\}A={2,325,9375,28178,450775,9780504,1795265022} 作底数时, 可保证 Miller-Rabin 在 2642^{64}264 内不会出错.

Code:

typedef long long ll;
ll qpow(int a,int e,int m)//快速幂, 计算a^e mod m
ll arr[7]={2,325,9375,28178,450775,9780504,1795265022};//用于探测的底数们
bool chkprm(ll x)//本体.
{
	if(x<3) {
        //特判:偶素数.
		return x==2;
	}
	if(x%2==0) {
        //特判:偶合数.
		return false;
	}
	ll d=x-1,r=0;
	while(d%2==0) {//分解x为2^r*d.
		d/=2;
		r++;
	}
	ll base;
	for(int i=0; i<7; i++) {
		base=qpow(arr[i],d,x);//base现记录a[i]^d. 即 a[i]^(2^0)*d
		if(base<=1||base==x-1) {
        /*如果base为0, x是a[i]的因数.
          如果base为1, 那么a[i]^(2^0)*d===1(mod x), 
          且接下来都是1, 属于情况(1).
          如果base为x-1, 那么a[i]^(2^0)*d===-1(mod x),
          且接下来都是1, 属于情况(2).*/
			continue;
		}
		for(int i=0; i<r; i++) {
			base=(__int128_t)base*base%x;
            //base自乘. 相当于计算数列的下一项.
			if(base==x-1&&i!=r-1) {
                //遇到了-1, 而且它不在结尾上. 那么接下来都是1, 属于情况(2).
				base=1;
                /*将base变为1(已知序列以1结尾), 再跳出 
                否则跳出后在46行的if处被误判为"以-1结尾".*/
				break;
			}
			if(base==1) {
                //遇到了1, 但是前面没有遇到-1(若经过-1则会在38行处跳出)
                //属于情况(3), 直接判定x非质数.
				return false;
			}
		}
		if(base!=1) {
            //结尾不是1. 属于情况(4), 判定x非质数.
			return false;
		}
	}
	return true;
}

II. Pollard-Rho算法分解质因数

如果需要找到 nnn 的一个因数, 试除法的期望复杂度大约是 O(n)O(\sqrt{n})O(n​), nnn 的规模过于夸张的话是不可接受的.

考虑用随机化提高试除的概率, 当选择一个数字 k∈Z+k\in\Z_+k∈Z+​, n,kn,kn,k 形成的最大公因数 (GCD) gcd⁡(n,k)\gcd(n,k)gcd(n,k) 只要不为 111 或 nnn ,就可以作为 nnn 的一个非平凡因数.

通过对家喻户晓的生日悖论进行研究, 可以发现, 选取数个随机数产生 nnn 的所有因数的概率很高.

Pollard设计了伪随机数列函数 f(x)=(x2+C)mod  nf(x)=(x^2+C)\mod nf(x)=(x2+C)modn, 其中 CCC 是一个随机生成的常数. Pollard-Rho 使用了该函数不断迭代(即将 f(x)f(x)f(x) 代入继续生成 f(f(x))f(f(x))f(f(x)), 如此类推)生成随机数, 并将相邻两项作差, 用这个差求 GCD.

f(x)f(x)f(x) 在迭代到一定次数后会进入循环. 类比一下希腊字母 ρ\rhoρ (rho, 算法名字的后半部分), 先生成一段数列, 而后生成内容被引向一个环.

此时寻找因数是没有意义的, 因此要引入判环算法帮助解决. Floyd 判环算法定义了一个乌龟变量和一个兔子变量, 分别记为 t,rt,rt,r. 每一轮迭代都使得 t←f(t),r←f(f(r))t\larr f(t), r\larr f(f(r))t←f(t),r←f(f(r)), 则如果进入环, rrr 和 ttt 的值会在某一点相遇, 于是我们更改常数 CCC, 重新寻找因数.

Code:

ll gcd(ll x,ll y)//返回x,y的最大公因数.
ll randint(ll L,ll r)//返回位于[L,R]的一个随机整数.
ll func(ll x,ll cst,ll mod)//f(x)函数, 参数cst表示常数C, 参数mod表示模数.
{
	return ((__int128_t)x*x+cst)%mod;
}
ll fact(ll x)//本体, 返回一个x的因数, 如果返回x, 说明没找到.
{
    ll rndnum=randint(1,x-1);//生成常数C!
    ll tur=0,rab=0;//乌龟变量, 兔子变量.
    while(t!=r)
    {
        ll _gcd=gcd(abs(tur-rab),x);//相邻两项作差, 求GCD.
        if(_gcd>1)
            return _gcd;
        tur=func(tur,rndnum,x),//乌龟跑一步.
        rab=func(func(rab,rndnum,x),rndnum,x);//兔子跑两步.
    }
    return x;//没找到, 返回重新生成常数C.
}

用辗转相除法求解 gcd⁡(x,y)\gcd(x,y)gcd(x,y) 的复杂度为 O(log⁡n)O(\log n)O(logn), 反复调用这个函数会使程序跑得很慢, 考虑到 GCD 可重复贡献的性质, 考虑将所有的 ∣t−r∣|t-r|∣t−r∣ 全部乘起来再求 GCD.

还有一个玄学的常数优化, 在迭代次数超过 128128128 而未退出环时考虑直接舍弃掉这个环, 直接生成新的常数参与运算.

Code:

ll fact(ll x)
{
	if(x==4)//特判4
	{
		return 2;
	}
	if(chkprm(x))//一定要先判断质数, 否则31处求得因数的值只能是1, 无法退出.
	{
		return x;
	}
	while(true)
	{
		ll rndnum=randint((ll)1,x-1);
		ll tur=0,rab=0;
		ll p=1,q;//配合记录GCD的乘积.
		do
		{
			for(int i=1;i<=128;i++)
			{
				tur=func(tur,rndnum,x),//乌龟跑一步.
                rab=func(func(rab,rndnum,x),rndnum,x);//兔子跑两步.
				q=(__int128_t)p*abs(tur-rab)%x;//求积.
				if(tur==rab||q==0)
                //tur==rab, 那么找到环; q==0, 那么积为0, 不产生贡献, 跳出.
				{
					break;
				}
				p=q;
			}
			ll _gcd=gcd(p,x);
			if(_gcd>1)
			{
				return _gcd; 
			}
		}while(tur!=rab);
	}
}

分解质因数时, 在外部加一个递归函数, 每次找到因数 kkk 就将 nnn 拆分为 nk\frac{n}{k}kn​ 和 kkk. 结合Miller-Rabin 判断两个因数的素性, 如果是素数, 停止分解后记录; 如果为合数, 继续向下递归分解.
